level_signals: Executive visibility and ability to set standards / define playbooks
Leads small cross-functional teams but no explicit people-management mandate

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of TPM experience, ideally in a high-growth startup environment
must_haves.qualifier: ideally in a high-growth startup environment

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Genuine AI fluency: you are actively building with and thinking about AI tools (Cursor, Claude Code, etc.) and using them in day-to-day work, not just aware of them
Ability to operate as both executor and leader. You're the person who does the work and raises the bar for how it gets done
Proven ability to earn trust quickly and become the person teams want to confide in — this is a relationship-driven role as much as an execution one
BA/BS in a technical or analytical field (e.g. Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, Analytics, or related)
Proven ability to lead small, cross-functional teams toward ambitious goals with urgency and agency.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building programs at high-rigor, fast-moving companies
Background as a software engineer or technical founder
Familiarity with how LLM-based systems are built and deployed — enough to have a meaningful technical conversation with the engineers building them

scope.surface: Engineering organization programs — CI/CD, infrastructure scaling, incident response, platform and product engineering coordination
primary_metrics_inferred: Engineering incident response quality and learning cadence
core_responsibilities: Lead large-scale technical programs across engineering and adjacent teams—from CI/CD and infrastructure scaling to incident response, and driving other strategic projects across the engineering organization
Own Ramp's engineering incident response program, improving processes, running retrospectives, and keeping the bar high on how the org learns from incidents

Be the connective tissue between platform and product engineering teams, ensuring context flows both ways and nothing falls through the cracks and high-quality execution of projects in an effective manner
Own programs end-to-end: from scoping and kickoff through delivery, observability, and retrospective
Partner with engineering leaders to define technical roadmaps, understand how multiple projects overlap and complement each other, and ensure the programs fit within the goals of the organization
Collaborate with lead engineers, engineering managers and other groups to help communicate business and engineering strategies to the wider organization
Help shape how the TPM function operates at Ramp: set standards, define playbooks, and raise the bar for how technical programs are run

green_flags: High executive visibility explicitly called out
Clear articulation of role scope and responsibilities
AI fluency is a genuine differentiator requirement, not just a checkbox — signals a forward-thinking engineering org
Relocation support to NYC or SF mentioned
Comprehensive benefits package across US, Canada, and UK
Role has real ownership mandate — 'end-to-end' program ownership
red_flags: Location type is ambiguous — JD mentions relocation to NYC or SF but does not clarify whether the role is remote, hybrid, or onsite
BA/BS credential listed without an 'equivalent experience' escape hatch, which may screen out capable candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required, executive visibility signals, and playbook-setting responsibilities)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description — B2B fintech spend management)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.type (unclear; relocation to NYC or SF mentioned but no explicit onsite/remote/hybrid policy stated)
ambiguities: Location type is not explicitly stated as remote, hybrid, or onsite. The JD mentions 'relocation support to NYC or SF' suggesting an in-office expectation, but this is not confirmed. Marked as 'unclear'.

The 'ideally in a high-growth startup environment' qualifier on the 5+ years TPM requirement is ambiguous — could be a soft preference or a hard filter. Treated as a hard requirement with the qualifier noted, since it appears in the 'What You Need' section.
Equity is not mentioned in the benefits section, which is unusual for a high-growth fintech; may be an omission rather than absence.
